intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable in the shared black pot. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, anchored by the white rabbit.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot appears empty/soil only)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Shared black pot contains eggshell debris; no viable plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and potential dehydration.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P2/P4).
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A small electronic component (likely a header pin connector) appears on the desk surface in later images, suggesting non-botanical intervention.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Gradual loss of light levels and potential desiccation of the remaining Pothos leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Severe light degradation; the scene is now in near-total darkness, rendering further visual assessment of plant turgor impossible.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. The lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 indicates total specimen loss. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor but exhibits poor health markers (loss of turgor, potential nutrient deficiency).
anomalies: Electronic component (header pins) introduced to the desk surface
Extreme lighting failure in the final frame
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failing indoor biome. The registry is outdated, as three of the four registered plants have likely perished. The remaining Pothos is struggling in a dry, neglected environment. The presence of eggshells suggests an amateur attempt at soil amendment, while the appearance of electronic debris indicates the desk is being used for non-botanical purposes, likely contributing to the neglect of the specimens.
